content: The Q&A session focused on clarifying the team's sustainability analysis and the viability of their proposed recommendations. The team explained that their competitive analysis was based on relative focus within each company's business model. They addressed concerns regarding the lack of visibility for sustainable options on the Agoda platform, distinguishing between environmental and social-cultural pillars. The discussion also covered the feasibility of the 'Agolocal' volunteer-based experience, with the team proposing a pilot program to test host and traveler engagement.
key_points: Competitor analysis was conducted by comparing relative focus areas within each company's existing business model.

The team identified a significant visibility gap, noting that 36% of travelers are unaware of existing sustainable initiatives, and even for those who are aware, finding these options requires excessive research.
Sustainability was segmented into three pillars: environmental, social, and cultural, with the team noting that Agoda currently lacks sufficient social-cultural offerings.
Recommendation one (Agolocal) aims to provide authentic, immersive experiences by partnering with locals, though reviewers questioned the sustainability and scalability of the volunteer-based model.
Reviewers emphasized the need for a stronger GTM strategy to drive initial user adoption of new platform features.
Financial and implementation slides were criticized for being too granular; the team was advised to focus on high-level drivers and key milestones.
action_items: Refine the GTM (Go-To-Market) strategy for recommendation two to clearly explain how to drive initial user awareness and traffic to the new eco-voyage tab.

Re-evaluate the sustainability framing of recommendation one to ensure it aligns with the three pillars of sustainability (environmental, social, and cultural) rather than relying solely on volunteer work.
Simplify implementation timelines by focusing only on 'Monday morning' actions and key evaluation stages rather than exhaustive step-by-step details.
Clarify revenue and cost drivers in financial projections instead of presenting generic growth charts.
Ensure recommendation details explicitly connect back to the target demographic of young travelers.
